refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into two key sub-functions: acquiring timely data from the worksite and processing that data efficiently to update the model.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure and verify the latency of data acquisition and processing separately and combined, ensuring the p95 latency is ≤ 1.0 s under supported update scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-frequency telemetry data streams with priority network scheduling
DP2: Implement edge computing for local data pre-processing
DP3: Optimize data processing algorithms for low-latency model updates
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP3 combined: Use high-frequency telemetry data streams with priority network scheduling and optimize data processing algorithms for low-latency model updates

potential_sources: Telemetry data acquisition tightly coupled with processing algorithms
Network link performance impacts both data acquisition and processing
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized to minimize cross-dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link supports priority scheduling and sufficient bandwidth
Risk of processing algorithm complexity causing latency spikes
Assumes telemetry data quality and frequency meet requirements
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding edge computing (DP2) if network constraints cause latency issues.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry data acquisition, processing, and worksite model update

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives high-frequency telemetry data streams from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports priority scheduling for telemetry data transmission and control messages

module_containment_scores.notes: Primary module responsible for data acquisition and processing; high containment and low diffusion
Provides telemetry data but does not process updates
May influence telemetry data but not responsible for processing
Not involved in worksite model update latency

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing telemetry data acquisition and processing within NGHE Solution module minimizes latency and diffusion of responsibility.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded
Dependency on network link performance external to NGHE Solution
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor module load and consider offloading processing to edge computing if latency targets are not met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are clearly addressed by the selected DPs, with logical mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Potential over-centralization in NGHE Solution module
Network link dependency may cause latency outside module control
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as the primary implementation module with clear interface boundaries.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring telemetry latency and processing times within NGHE Solution using known interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of FRs to NGHE Solution module is clear and aligns with stakeholder expectations.

recommended_revisions: Evaluate adding edge computing module if latency targets are not consistently met.
Clarify network link performance requirements with stakeholders.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the latency requirement with clear rationale and manageable risks. Verification approaches are feasible and ownership is well defined.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints

revise.why: No immediate revisions needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network link supports priority scheduling and sufficient bandwidth
Telemetry data quality and frequency meet requirements
open_risks: Processing algorithm complexity causing latency spikes
Potential bottleneck in NGHE Solution module
evidence_needed: Latency measurements of telemetry data acquisition and processing
Network link performance statistics under operational conditions
